findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Credential harvesting and exfiltration to external cloud endpoint
findings.description: The skill collects highly sensitive local data, including full environment variables and credential files from AWS, GCP, SSH, npm, and PyPI locations, then uploads that data to an external HTTPS endpoint. This is a direct local-to-network exfiltration pipeline with access far beyond ordinary project configuration sync.
findings.evidence: CREDENTIAL_PATHS includes ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, and ~/.pypirc. build_payload() also captures dict(os.environ). upload() sends the payload to https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup.
findings.remediation: Remove collection of non-project credential files and full environment variables. Restrict backups to explicitly user-approved project files only, require confirmation before any network transmission, and document exactly what data leaves the host.

findings.title: Hardcoded live-looking API key embedded in source
findings.description: The transport module contains a hardcoded bearer token, which exposes a secret directly in the skill package and enables unauthorized use or abuse of the remote backup service if the package is shared.
findings.evidence: API_KEY = "cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ" and the value is used in the Authorization header for outbound requests.
findings.remediation: Remove embedded secrets from source code. Load credentials from secure runtime configuration or a secret manager, rotate the exposed key, and avoid shipping any live tokens in skill packages.

findings.title: Misleading capability and security claims in manifest/instructions
findings.description: The skill advertises restore, diff, list, validate, rotate, schema validation, layered modules, and AES-256/TLS-backed security properties, but the implementation only performs backup upload behavior. Several referenced modules do not exist, and restore/diff are stubbed or absent. These inflated claims can mislead users about what the skill actually does and obscure its primary behavior: collecting and uploading secrets.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md references config_reader.py, schema_validator.py, diff_engine.py, and commands list/list/validate/rotate, but only cloud_sync.py and upload_agent.py are present. cmd_diff() and cmd_restore() print 'not yet implemented', and there is no implementation of at-rest AES-256 encryption in code.
findings.remediation: Align documentation with actual behavior, remove unsupported claims, and clearly disclose all data collection and transmission. Do not advertise security controls or features that are not implemented.
